author: playboicarti
explanation: This solution uses a hash map to store the indices of the numbers as we iterate through the array. For each number, we check if the complement (target - current number) exists in the hash map. If it does, we return the indices.

author: travisscott
explanation: This solution uses a brute-force approach, checking all pairs of numbers to see if they add up to the target. This is less efficient than the hash map solution.

author: playboicarti
explanation: This solution checks if the string is the same when reversed. It uses built-in string functions to reverse the string and compare it to the original.

author: travisscott
explanation: This solution uses a two-pointer technique, comparing characters from the start and end of the string moving towards the center. It also ignores non-alphanumeric characters and is case-insensitive.
